message:
fix(patterns): version command must include persisted customs (#2 follow-on)

Caught in the third fresh-eyes pass: `slb patterns version` was
reading `engine.GetDefaultEngine()` directly without the loader,
so it always reported the builtins-only sha256 and pattern_count
even after `slb patterns add` had persisted customs. Tooling
that uses the hash to decide "do I need to regenerate the hook?"
would silently miss every customization.

This was the last remaining hole in the engine-uses-loader
contract. Audit (excluding self-uses inside the loader and
patternsAddCmd's mutation path):

  - patternsTestCmd        - load before classify     ✓ (yesterday)
  - patternsListCmd        - load before AllPatterns  ✓ (yesterday)
  - patternsExportCmd      - load before export       ✓ (yesterday)
  - patternsVersionCmd     - load before export       ✗ THIS FIX
  - runHookGenerate        - load before generate     ✓ (today)
  - runHookInstall         - load before generate     ✓ (today)
  - runHookTest            - load before classify     ✓ (today)
  - runHookStatus          - load before hash report  ✓ (today)
  - daemon RunDaemon       - load at startup          ✓ (today)
  - daemon handleHookHealth - reads engine post-load  ✓ (covered)

Regression test:
TestPatternsVersionCommand_ReflectsPersistedCustomPatterns —
takes a baseline (builtins only), persists a unique custom
pattern, re-reads version, asserts pattern_count went up by
exactly 1 AND the sha256 differs from baseline. Without the
loader call, both assertions fail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -521,6 +521,14 @@ Examples:
   slb patterns version        # Show version info
   slb patterns version -j     # JSON output`,
 	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
+		// Reflect persisted customs in the reported sha256 +
+		// pattern_count. Without this, `patterns version` would
+		// always report the builtins-only hash even after a user
+		// has added customs — and that hash is what tooling uses
+		// to decide whether to regenerate the hook script.
+		if _, err := loadCustomPatternsIntoDefaultEngine(); err != nil {
+			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "warning: %v\n", err)
+		}
 		engine := core.GetDefaultEngine()
 		export := engine.Export()
